from notebookutils import mssparkutils
from datetime import datetime, date
import pyspark.sql.functions as F

%%sql
CREATE OR REPLACE  VIEW odw_curated_db.vw_Appeal_event_curated_mipins
AS
SELECT
    eventId
    ,caseReference
    ,eventType
    ,eventName
    ,eventStatus
    ,CAST(IsUrgent AS Boolean) AS IsUrgent
    ,CAST(eventPublished AS Boolean) AS eventPublished
    ,CAST(eventStartDateTime AS Timestamp) AS eventStartDateTime
    ,CAST(eventEndDateTime AS Timestamp)  AS eventEndDateTime
    ,CAST(NotificationOfSiteVisit AS Timestamp) AS NotificationOfSiteVisit
    ,addressLine1
    ,addressLine2
    ,addressTown
    ,addressCounty
    ,addressPostcode
    ,Migrated
    ,ODTSourceSystem
    ,SourceSystemID
    ,IngestionDate
    ,ValidTo
    ,RowID
    ,IsActive
FROM
    odw_harmonised_db.appeal_event   
WHERE
    ODTSourceSystem = 'ODT'
AND (eventStartDateTime IS NULL OR eventStartDateTime >= TIMESTAMP('1900-01-01'))
AND (eventEndDateTime IS NULL OR eventEndDateTime >= TIMESTAMP('1900-01-01'))
AND (NotificationOfSiteVisit IS NULL OR NotificationOfSiteVisit >= TIMESTAMP('1900-01-01'))
AND (IngestionDate IS NULL OR IngestionDate >= TIMESTAMP('1900-01-01'))
AND (ValidTo IS NULL OR ValidTo >= TIMESTAMP('1900-01-01'))

# prepare dataframe convert UTC to Day light savings for all timestamp fields

# List of UTC timestamp columns to convert
timestamp_columns = [
'eventStartDateTime',    
'eventEndDateTime', 
'IngestionDate',
'ValidTo',

]

# Apply the UTC to London conversion for each column
final_view_df = view_df
for column_name in timestamp_columns:
    final_view_df = final_view_df.withColumn(column_name, F.to_timestamp(F.from_utc_timestamp(F.col(column_name), 'Europe/London'), "yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss.SSS"))
